topics._comment: Array of lowercase topic tags. Used by agent skills for filtering and selection. The CLI does not read this field.

difficulty._comment: All values 1-5. overall = (algorithmComplexity * 0.3) + (dataStructureComplexity * 0.3) + (problemComplexity * 0.4), rounded to nearest integer.

style._comment: Problem style. Valid values: 'leetcode' (algorithmic, well-defined inputs/outputs) or 'real-world' (practical scenario, open-ended design). Used by agent skills for generation targeting.

expectedMinutes._comment: Suggested time limit in minutes. Pre-populates the countdown prompt at session start. Agent skills set this based on difficulty and part count.

generatedBy._comment: Origin of the problem. 'manual' for hand-authored problems, 'agent' for AI-generated problems. The CLI does not read this field.

generatedAt._comment: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the problem was generated. Empty string for manually authored problems. Set automatically by the handwritten-generate skill.

parts._comment: Ordered list of part objects. Must contain at least one entry. maxPartsGlobal in config.json caps the number of parts agent skills will generate, but the CLI itself imposes no limit.

activeTests._comment: Test names to run when this part is active. Must be non-empty. Each entry must exactly match the test('...') string in Jest and the function name (minus test_ prefix, with underscores for spaces) in pytest.

scaffold._comment: Starter code injected into the workspace file. Part 1 scaffold is written as the full file. Part 2+ scaffolds are appended after a delimiter comment: '// ---- Part N ----' (JS) or '# ---- Part N ----' (Python).

runInputs._comment: Optional. 2-3 representative function calls executed on every save and shown as Examples before solving. Per-part — accumulates as parts unlock. Language-specific entries. Args and expected must be JSON-serializable. Expected is optional but strongly recommended — omitting it shows return values with no pass/fail indicator. Labels appear in console panel and problem description. Do not duplicate test inputs exactly. Async functions are handled automatically — no special configuration required.

value.label: <string: short scenario name, e.g. 'basic case'>
value.function: <string: exact exported function name matching scaffold>
value.expected: <any JSON-serializable value — omit field entirely if unknown>

value.label: <string: different scenario>
value.function: <string: exact exported function name matching scaffold>

activeTests._comment: Include prior part tests here if they should continue running. activeTests is NOT automatically cumulative.

scaffold._comment: Use additive exports in JS (module.exports.newFn = newFn) to avoid overwriting Part 1 exports. Empty string is valid if no new scaffold is needed.

runInputs._comment: Part 2+ run inputs supplement Part 1 inputs. All unlocked parts' run inputs execute on every save. Choose inputs that exercise the new function introduced in this part.

value.label: <string: scenario for part 2 function>
value.function: <string: exact exported function name from this part's scaffold>
